subject: Mathematics
title: Spurious Correlations
description: A fast breakdown of Spurious Correlations for builders.

hook.text: The number of films Nicolas Cage appeared in correlates with swimming pool drownings. Coincidence is everywhere.

buildup.text: With millions of datasets available, you can always find two unrelated things that move in sync.

discovery.text: These spurious correlations arise from coincidence, shared trends, or common underlying factors.

twist.text: Data mining—searching thousands of variables for correlations—guarantees you'll find some fake ones.

climax.text: This is why scientists require hypotheses before collecting data, not after finding convenient patterns.

punchline.text: If you look hard enough, you'll always find a pattern—even a meaningless one.

quiz.question: Why are spurious correlations dangerous?
options: They can lead to false conclusions about cause and effect
They only appear in medical research
